annotations.note: Gaussian density function (up to reparametrization) is defined as gaussianPDFReal in Mathlib.
Independence of two sets is characterised exactly by μ(A ∩ B) = μ A · μ B.
Trivial consequence of Disjoint sets having empty intersection and measure_empty; no standalone lemma.
Additivity on disjoint measurable sets: μ(s₁ ∪ s₂) = μ s₁ + μ s₂.
Two-set inclusion-exclusion μ(s ∪ t) + μ(s ∩ t) = μ s + μ t.
Same identity rewritten as μ(A ∪ B) = μ A + μ B − μ(A ∩ B).

anchor.value: {\displaystyle {\begin{aligned}P\left(A\cup B\cup C\right)=&P\left(\left(A\cup B\right)\cup C\right)\\=&P\left(A\cup B\right)+P\left(C\right)-P\left(\left(A\cup B\right)\cap C\right)\\=&P\left(A\right)+P\left(B\right)-P\left(A\cap B\right)+P\left(C\right)-P\left(\left(A\cap C\right)\cap \left(B\cap C\right)\right)\\=&P\left(A\right)+P\left(B\right)+P\left(C\right)-P\left(A\cap B\right)-\left(P\left(A\cap C\right)+P\left(B\cap C\right)-P\left(\left(A\cap C\right)\cap \left(B\cap C\right)\right)\right)\\P\left(A\cup B\cup C\right)=&P\left(A\right)+P\left(B\right)+P\left(C\right)-P\left(A\cap B\right)-P\left(A\cap C\right)-P\left(B\cap C\right)+P\left(A\cap B\cap C\right)\end{aligned}}}

annotations.note: Three-set measure inclusion-exclusion is not a named lemma but derivable by iterating measure_union_add_inter.
Conditional probability is defined so that μ[t | s] = (μ s)⁻¹ · μ(s ∩ t).
